2208 proc format*(dt: DateTime, f: string, loc: DateTimeLocale = DefaultLocale): string
2209 {.raises: [TimeFormatParseError].} =
2210 ## Shorthand for constructing a `TimeFormat` and using it to format `dt`.
2211 2 ##
2212 2 ## See `Parsing and formatting dates`_ for documentation of the
2213 ## `format` argument.
2214 runnableExamples:
2215 let dt = dateTime(2000, mJan, 01, 00, 00, 00, 00, utc())
2216 doAssert "2000-01-01" == format(dt, "yyyy-MM-dd")
2217 let dtFormat = initTimeFormat(f)
2218 result = dt.format(dtFormat, loc)
2219
2220 2 proc format*(dt: DateTime, f: static[string]): string {.raises: [].} =
2221 ## Overload that validates `format` at compile time.
2222 const f2 = initTimeFormat(f)
2223
2/4
✗ Branch 0 not taken.
✓ Branch 1 taken 1 time.
✗ Branch 2 not taken.
✓ Branch 3 taken 1 time.
 2 result = dt.format(f2)
2224 2
2225 proc formatValue*(result: var string; value: DateTime | Time, specifier: string) =
2226 ## adapter for strformat. Not intended to be called directly.
2227 result.add format(value,
2228 if specifier.len == 0: "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:sszzz" else: specifier)
2229
2230 proc format*(time: Time, f: string, zone: Timezone = local()): string
2231 {.raises: [TimeFormatParseError].} =
2232 ## Shorthand for constructing a `TimeFormat` and using it to format
2233 ## `time`. Will use the timezone specified by `zone`.
2234 ##
2235 ## See `Parsing and formatting dates`_ for documentation of the
2236 ## `f` argument.
2237 runnableExamples:
2238 var dt = dateTime(1970, mJan, 01, 00, 00, 00, 00, utc())
2239 var tm = dt.toTime()
2240 doAssert format(tm, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss", utc()) == "1970-01-01T00:00:00"
2241 time.inZone(zone).format(f)
2242
2243 proc format*(time: Time, f: static[string], zone: Timezone = local()): string
2244 {.raises: [].} =
2245 ## Overload that validates `f` at compile time.
2246 const f2 = initTimeFormat(f)
2247 result = time.inZone(zone).format(f2)
2248
2249 proc parse*(input: string, f: TimeFormat, zone: Timezone = local(),
2250 loc: DateTimeLocale = DefaultLocale): DateTime {.parseRaises.} =
2251 ## Parses `input` as a `DateTime` using the format specified by `f`.
2252 ## If no UTC offset was parsed, then `input` is assumed to be specified in
2253 ## the `zone` timezone. If a UTC offset was parsed, the result will be
2254 ## converted to the `zone` timezone.
2255 ##
2256 ## Month and day names from the passed in `loc` are used.
2257 runnableExamples:
2258 let f = initTimeFormat("yyyy-MM-dd")
2259 let dt = dateTime(2000, mJan, 01, 00, 00, 00, 00, utc())
2260 doAssert dt == "2000-01-01".parse(f, utc())
2261 result = default(DateTime)
2262 var inpIdx = 0 # Input index
2263 var patIdx = 0 # Pattern index
2264 var parsed: ParsedTime = default(ParsedTime)
2265 while inpIdx <= input.high and patIdx <= f.patterns.high:
2266 let pattern = f.patterns[patIdx].FormatPattern
2267 case pattern
2268 of Lit:
2269 patIdx.inc
2270 let len = f.patterns[patIdx]
2271 patIdx.inc
2272 for _ in 1'u8..len:
2273 if input[inpIdx] != f.patterns[patIdx].char:
2274 raiseParseException(f, input,
2275 "Unexpected character: " & input[inpIdx])
2276 inpIdx.inc
2277 patIdx.inc
2278 else:
2279 if not parsePattern(input, pattern, inpIdx, parsed, loc):
2280 raiseParseException(f, input, "Failed on pattern '" & $pattern & "'")
2281 patIdx.inc
2282
2283 if inpIdx <= input.high:
2284 raiseParseException(f, input,
2285 "Parsing ended but there was still input remaining")
2286
2287 if patIdx <= f.patterns.high:
2288 raiseParseException(f, input,
2289 "Parsing ended but there was still patterns remaining")
2290
2291 if parsed.yearweek.isSome:
2292 result = toDateTimeByWeek(parsed, zone, f, input)
2293 elif parsed.isoyear.isSome:
2294 raiseParseException(f, input, "Iso year GG or GGGG require iso week V or VV")
2295 else:
2296 result = toDateTime(parsed, zone, f, input)
2297
2298 proc parse*(input, f: string, tz: Timezone = local(),
2299 loc: DateTimeLocale = DefaultLocale): DateTime {.parseFormatRaises.} =
2300 ## Shorthand for constructing a `TimeFormat` and using it to parse
2301 ## `input` as a `DateTime`.
2302 ##
2303 ## See `Parsing and formatting dates`_ for documentation of the
2304 ## `f` argument.
2305 runnableExamples:
2306 let dt = dateTime(2000, mJan, 01, 00, 00, 00, 00, utc())
2307 doAssert dt == parse("2000-01-01", "yyyy-MM-dd", utc())
2308 let dtFormat = initTimeFormat(f)
2309 result = input.parse(dtFormat, tz, loc = loc)
2310
2311 proc parse*(input: string, f: static[string], zone: Timezone = local(),
2312 loc: DateTimeLocale = DefaultLocale): DateTime {.parseRaises.} =
2313 ## Overload that validates `f` at compile time.
2314 const f2 = initTimeFormat(f)
2315 result = input.parse(f2, zone, loc = loc)
2316
2317 proc parseTime*(input, f: string, zone: Timezone): Time {.parseFormatRaises.} =
2318 ## Shorthand for constructing a `TimeFormat` and using it to parse
2319 ## `input` as a `DateTime`, then converting it a `Time`.
2320 ##
2321 ## See `Parsing and formatting dates`_ for documentation of the
2322 ## `format` argument.
2323 runnableExamples:
2324 let tStr = "1970-01-01T00:00:00+00:00"
2325 doAssert parseTime(tStr, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:sszzz", utc()) == fromUnix(0)
2326 parse(input, f, zone).toTime()
2327
2328 proc parseTime*(input: string, f: static[string], zone: Timezone): Time
2329 {.parseRaises.} =
2330 ## Overload that validates `format` at compile time.
2331 const f2 = initTimeFormat(f)
2332 result = input.parse(f2, zone).toTime()
2333
2334 proc `$`*(dt: DateTime): string {.tags: [], raises: [], gcsafe.} =
2335 ## Converts a `DateTime` object to a string representation.
2336 ## It uses the format `yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:sszzz`.
2337 runnableExamples:
2338 let dt = dateTime(2000, mJan, 01, 12, 00, 00, 00, utc())
2339 doAssert $dt == "2000-01-01T12:00:00Z"
2340 doAssert $default(DateTime) == "Uninitialized DateTime"
2341 if not dt.isInitialized:
2342 result = "Uninitialized DateTime"
2343 else:
2344 result = format(dt, "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:sszzz")
2345
2346 proc `$`*(time: Time): string {.tags: [], raises: [], gcsafe.} =
2347 ## Converts a `Time` value to a string representation. It will use the local
2348 ## time zone and use the format `yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:sszzz`.
2349 runnableExamples:
2350 let dt = dateTime(1970, mJan, 01, 00, 00, 00, 00, local())
2351 let tm = dt.toTime()
2352 doAssert $tm == "1970-01-01T00:00:00" & format(dt, "zzz")
2353 $time.local
2354
2355 #
2356 # TimeInterval
2357 #
2358
2359 proc initTimeInterval*(nanoseconds = 0, microseconds = 0, milliseconds = 0,
2360 seconds = 0, minutes = 0, hours = 0,
2361 days = 0, weeks = 0, months = 0, years = 0): TimeInterval =
2362 ## Creates a new `TimeInterval <#TimeInterval>`_.
2363 ##
2364 ## This proc doesn't perform any normalization! For example,
2365 ## `initTimeInterval(hours = 24)` and `initTimeInterval(days = 1)` are
2366 ## not equal.
2367 ##
2368 ## You can also use the convenience procedures called `milliseconds`,
2369 ## `seconds`, `minutes`, `hours`, `days`, `months`, and `years`.
2370 runnableExamples:
2371 let day = initTimeInterval(hours = 24)
2372 let dt = dateTime(2000, mJan, 01, 12, 00, 00, 00, utc())
2373 doAssert $(dt + day) == "2000-01-02T12:00:00Z"
2374 doAssert initTimeInterval(hours = 24) != initTimeInterval(days = 1)
2375 result = TimeInterval(
2376 nanoseconds: nanoseconds,
2377 microseconds: microseconds,
2378 milliseconds: milliseconds,
2379 seconds: seconds,
2380 minutes: minutes,
2381 hours: hours,
2382 days: days,
2383 weeks: weeks,
2384 months: months,
2385 years: years
2386 )
2387
2388 proc `+`*(ti1, ti2: TimeInterval): TimeInterval =
2389 ## Adds two `TimeInterval` objects together.
2390 result = TimeInterval(
2391 nanoseconds: ti1.nanoseconds + ti2.nanoseconds,
2392 microseconds: ti1.microseconds + ti2.microseconds,
2393 milliseconds: ti1.milliseconds + ti2.milliseconds,
2394 seconds: ti1.seconds + ti2.seconds,
2395 minutes: ti1.minutes + ti2.minutes,
2396 hours: ti1.hours + ti2.hours,
2397 days: ti1.days + ti2.days,
2398 weeks: ti1.weeks + ti2.weeks,
2399 months: ti1.months + ti2.months,
2400 years: ti1.years + ti2.years
2401 )
2402
2403 proc `-`*(ti: TimeInterval): TimeInterval =
2404 ## Reverses a time interval
2405 runnableExamples:
2406 let day = -initTimeInterval(hours = 24)
2407 doAssert day.hours == -24
2408
2409 result = TimeInterval(
2410 nanoseconds: -ti.nanoseconds,
2411 microseconds: -ti.microseconds,
2412 milliseconds: -ti.milliseconds,
2413 seconds: -ti.seconds,
2414 minutes: -ti.minutes,
2415 hours: -ti.hours,
2416 days: -ti.days,
2417 weeks: -ti.weeks,
2418 months: -ti.months,
2419 years: -ti.years
2420 )
2421
2422 proc `-`*(ti1, ti2: TimeInterval): TimeInterval =
2423 ## Subtracts TimeInterval `ti1` from `ti2`.
2424 ##
2425 ## Time components are subtracted one-by-one, see output:
2426 runnableExamples:
2427 let ti1 = initTimeInterval(hours = 24)
2428 let ti2 = initTimeInterval(hours = 4)
2429 doAssert (ti1 - ti2) == initTimeInterval(hours = 20)
2430
2431 result = ti1 + (-ti2)
2432
2433 proc `+=`*(a: var TimeInterval, b: TimeInterval) =
2434 a = a + b
2435
2436 proc `-=`*(a: var TimeInterval, b: TimeInterval) =
2437 a = a - b
2438
2439 proc isStaticInterval(interval: TimeInterval): bool =
2440 interval.years == 0 and interval.months == 0 and
2441 interval.days == 0 and interval.weeks == 0
2442
2443 proc evaluateStaticInterval(interval: TimeInterval): Duration =
2444 assert interval.isStaticInterval
2445 initDuration(nanoseconds = interval.nanoseconds,
2446 microseconds = interval.microseconds,
2447 milliseconds = interval.milliseconds,
2448 seconds = interval.seconds,
2449 minutes = interval.minutes,
2450 hours = interval.hours)
2451
2452 proc between*(startDt, endDt: DateTime): TimeInterval =
2453 ## Gives the difference between `startDt` and `endDt` as a
2454 ## `TimeInterval`. The following guarantees about the result is given:
2455 ##
2456 ## - All fields will have the same sign.
2457 ## - If `startDt.timezone == endDt.timezone`, it is guaranteed that
2458 ## `startDt + between(startDt, endDt) == endDt`.
2459 ## - If `startDt.timezone != endDt.timezone`, then the result will be
2460 ## equivalent to `between(startDt.utc, endDt.utc)`.
2461 runnableExamples:
2462 var a = dateTime(2015, mMar, 25, 12, 0, 0, 00, utc())
2463 var b = dateTime(2017, mApr, 1, 15, 0, 15, 00, utc())
2464 var ti = initTimeInterval(years = 2, weeks = 1, hours = 3, seconds = 15)
2465 doAssert between(a, b) == ti
2466 doAssert between(a, b) == -between(b, a)
2467
2468 result = default(TimeInterval)
2469 if startDt.timezone != endDt.timezone:
2470 return between(startDt.utc, endDt.utc)
2471 elif endDt < startDt:
2472 return -between(endDt, startDt)
2473
2474 type Date = tuple[year, month, monthday: int]
2475 var startDate: Date = (startDt.year, startDt.month.ord, startDt.monthday)
2476 var endDate: Date = (endDt.year, endDt.month.ord, endDt.monthday)
2477
2478 # Subtract one day from endDate if time of day is earlier than startDay
2479 # The subtracted day will be counted by fixed units (hour and lower)
2480 # at the end of this proc
2481 if (endDt.hour, endDt.minute, endDt.second, endDt.nanosecond) <
2482 (startDt.hour, startDt.minute, startDt.second, startDt.nanosecond):
2483 if endDate.month == 1 and endDate.monthday == 1:
2484 endDate.year.dec
2485 endDate.monthday = 31
2486 endDate.month = 12
2487 elif endDate.monthday == 1:
2488 endDate.month.dec
2489 endDate.monthday = getDaysInMonth(endDate.month.Month, endDate.year)
2490 else:
2491 endDate.monthday.dec
2492
2493 # Years
2494 result.years = endDate.year - startDate.year - 1
2495 if (startDate.month, startDate.monthday) <= (endDate.month, endDate.monthday):
2496 result.years.inc
2497 startDate.year.inc result.years
2498
2499 # Months
2500 if startDate.year < endDate.year:
2501 result.months.inc 12 - startDate.month # Move to dec
2502 if endDate.month != 1 or (startDate.monthday <= endDate.monthday):
2503 result.months.inc
2504 startDate.year = endDate.year
2505 startDate.month = 1
2506 else:
2507 startDate.month = 12
2508 if startDate.year == endDate.year:
2509 if (startDate.monthday <= endDate.monthday):
2510 result.months.inc endDate.month - startDate.month
2511 startDate.month = endDate.month
2512 elif endDate.month != 1:
2513 let month = endDate.month - 1
2514 let daysInMonth = getDaysInMonth(month.Month, startDate.year)
2515 if daysInMonth < startDate.monthday:
2516 if startDate.monthday - daysInMonth < endDate.monthday:
2517 result.months.inc endDate.month - startDate.month - 1
2518 startDate.month = endDate.month
2519 startDate.monthday = startDate.monthday - daysInMonth
2520 else:
2521 result.months.inc endDate.month - startDate.month - 2
2522 startDate.month = endDate.month - 2
2523 else:
2524 result.months.inc endDate.month - startDate.month - 1
2525 startDate.month = endDate.month - 1
2526
2527 # Days
2528 # This means that start = dec and end = jan
2529 if startDate.year < endDate.year:
2530 result.days.inc 31 - startDate.monthday + endDate.monthday
2531 startDate = endDate
2532 else:
2533 while startDate.month < endDate.month:
2534 let daysInMonth = getDaysInMonth(startDate.month.Month, startDate.year)
2535 result.days.inc daysInMonth - startDate.monthday + 1
2536 startDate.month.inc
2537 startDate.monthday = 1
2538 result.days.inc endDate.monthday - startDate.monthday
2539 result.weeks = result.days div 7
2540 result.days = result.days mod 7
2541 startDate = endDate
2542
2543 # Handle hours, minutes, seconds, milliseconds, microseconds and nanoseconds
2544 let newStartDt = dateTime(startDate.year, startDate.month.Month,
2545 startDate.monthday, startDt.hour, startDt.minute, startDt.second,
2546 startDt.nanosecond, startDt.timezone)
2547 let dur = endDt - newStartDt
2548 let parts = toParts(dur)
2549 # There can still be a full day in `parts` since `Duration` and `TimeInterval`
2550 # models days differently.
2551 result.hours = parts[Hours].int + parts[Days].int * 24
2552 result.minutes = parts[Minutes].int
2553 result.seconds = parts[Seconds].int
2554 result.milliseconds = parts[Milliseconds].int
2555 result.microseconds = parts[Microseconds].int
2556 result.nanoseconds = parts[Nanoseconds].int
2557
2558 proc toParts*(ti: TimeInterval): TimeIntervalParts =
2559 ## Converts a `TimeInterval` into an array consisting of its time units,
2560 ## starting with nanoseconds and ending with years.
2561 ##
2562 ## This procedure is useful for converting `TimeInterval` values to strings.
2563 ## E.g. then you need to implement custom interval printing
2564 runnableExamples:
2565 var tp = toParts(initTimeInterval(years = 1, nanoseconds = 123))
2566 doAssert tp[Years] == 1
2567 doAssert tp[Nanoseconds] == 123
2568 result = default(TimeIntervalParts)
2569 var index = 0
2570 for name, value in fieldPairs(ti):
2571 result[index.TimeUnit()] = value
2572 index += 1
2573
2574 proc `$`*(ti: TimeInterval): string =
2575 ## Get string representation of `TimeInterval`.
2576 runnableExamples:
2577 doAssert $initTimeInterval(years = 1, nanoseconds = 123) ==
2578 "1 year and 123 nanoseconds"
2579 doAssert $initTimeInterval() == "0 nanoseconds"
2580
2581 var parts: seq[string] = @[]
2582 var tiParts = toParts(ti)
2583 for unit in countdown(Years, Nanoseconds):
2584 if tiParts[unit] != 0:
2585 parts.add(stringifyUnit(tiParts[unit], unit))
2586
2587 result = humanizeParts(parts)
2588
2589 proc nanoseconds*(nanos: int): TimeInterval {.inline.} =
2590 ## TimeInterval of `nanos` nanoseconds.
2591 initTimeInterval(nanoseconds = nanos)
2592
2593 proc microseconds*(micros: int): TimeInterval {.inline.} =
2594 ## TimeInterval of `micros` microseconds.
2595 initTimeInterval(microseconds = micros)
2596
2597 proc milliseconds*(ms: int): TimeInterval {.inline.} =
2598 ## TimeInterval of `ms` milliseconds.
2599 initTimeInterval(milliseconds = ms)
2600
2601 proc seconds*(s: int): TimeInterval {.inline.} =
2602 ## TimeInterval of `s` seconds.
2603 ##
2604 ## `echo getTime() + 5.seconds`
2605 initTimeInterval(seconds = s)
2606
2607 proc minutes*(m: int): TimeInterval {.inline.} =
2608 ## TimeInterval of `m` minutes.
2609 ##
2610 ## `echo getTime() + 5.minutes`
2611 initTimeInterval(minutes = m)
2612
2613 proc hours*(h: int): TimeInterval {.inline.} =
2614 ## TimeInterval of `h` hours.
2615 ##
2616 ## `echo getTime() + 2.hours`
2617 initTimeInterval(hours = h)
2618
2619 proc days*(d: int): TimeInterval {.inline.} =
2620 ## TimeInterval of `d` days.
2621 ##
2622 ## `echo getTime() + 2.days`
2623 initTimeInterval(days = d)
2624
2625 proc weeks*(w: int): TimeInterval {.inline.} =
2626 ## TimeInterval of `w` weeks.
2627 ##
2628 ## `echo getTime() + 2.weeks`
2629 initTimeInterval(weeks = w)
2630
2631 proc months*(m: int): TimeInterval {.inline.} =
2632 ## TimeInterval of `m` months.
2633 ##
2634 ## `echo getTime() + 2.months`
2635 initTimeInterval(months = m)
2636
2637 proc years*(y: int): TimeInterval {.inline.} =
2638 ## TimeInterval of `y` years.
2639 ##
2640 ## `echo getTime() + 2.years`
2641 initTimeInterval(years = y)
2642
2643 proc evaluateInterval(dt: DateTime, interval: TimeInterval):
2644 tuple[adjDur, absDur: Duration] =
2645 ## Evaluates how many nanoseconds the interval is worth
2646 ## in the context of `dt`.
2647 ## The result in split into an adjusted diff and an absolute diff.
2648 var months = interval.years * 12 + interval.months
2649 var curYear = dt.year
2650 var curMonth = dt.month
2651 result = default(tuple[adjDur, absDur: Duration])
2652 # Subtracting
2653 if months < 0:
2654 for mth in countdown(-1 * months, 1):
2655 if curMonth == mJan:
2656 curMonth = mDec
2657 curYear.dec
2658 else:
2659 curMonth.dec()
2660 let days = getDaysInMonth(curMonth, curYear)
2661 result.adjDur = result.adjDur - initDuration(days = days)
2662 # Adding
2663 else:
2664 for mth in 1 .. months:
2665 let days = getDaysInMonth(curMonth, curYear)
2666 result.adjDur = result.adjDur + initDuration(days = days)
2667 if curMonth == mDec:
2668 curMonth = mJan
2669 curYear.inc
2670 else:
2671 curMonth.inc()
2672
2673 result.adjDur = result.adjDur + initDuration(
2674 days = interval.days,
2675 weeks = interval.weeks)
2676 result.absDur = initDuration(
2677 nanoseconds = interval.nanoseconds,
2678 microseconds = interval.microseconds,
2679 milliseconds = interval.milliseconds,
2680 seconds = interval.seconds,
2681 minutes = interval.minutes,
2682 hours = interval.hours)
2683
2684 proc `+`*(dt: DateTime, interval: TimeInterval): DateTime =
2685 ## Adds `interval` to `dt`. Components from `interval` are added
2686 ## in the order of their size, i.e. first the `years` component, then the
2687 ## `months` component and so on. The returned `DateTime` will have the
2688 ## same timezone as the input.
2689 ##
2690 ## Note that when adding months, monthday overflow is allowed. This means that
2691 ## if the resulting month doesn't have enough days it, the month will be
2692 ## incremented and the monthday will be set to the number of days overflowed.
2693 ## So adding one month to `31 October` will result in `31 November`, which
2694 ## will overflow and result in `1 December`.
2695 runnableExamples:
2696 let dt = dateTime(2017, mMar, 30, 00, 00, 00, 00, utc())
2697 doAssert $(dt + 1.months) == "2017-04-30T00:00:00Z"
2698 # This is correct and happens due to monthday overflow.
2699 doAssert $(dt - 1.months) == "2017-03-02T00:00:00Z"
2700 let (adjDur, absDur) = evaluateInterval(dt, interval)
2701
2702 if adjDur != DurationZero:
2703 var zt = dt.timezone.zonedTimeFromAdjTime(dt.toAdjTime + adjDur)
2704 if absDur != DurationZero:
2705 zt = dt.timezone.zonedTimeFromTime(zt.time + absDur)
2706 result = initDateTime(zt, dt.timezone)
2707 else:
2708 result = initDateTime(zt, dt.timezone)
2709 else:
2710 var zt = dt.timezone.zonedTimeFromTime(dt.toTime + absDur)
2711 result = initDateTime(zt, dt.timezone)
2712
2713 proc `-`*(dt: DateTime, interval: TimeInterval): DateTime =
2714 ## Subtract `interval` from `dt`. Components from `interval` are
2715 ## subtracted in the order of their size, i.e. first the `years` component,
2716 ## then the `months` component and so on. The returned `DateTime` will
2717 ## have the same timezone as the input.
2718 runnableExamples:
2719 let dt = dateTime(2017, mMar, 30, 00, 00, 00, 00, utc())
2720 doAssert $(dt - 5.days) == "2017-03-25T00:00:00Z"
2721
2722 dt + (-interval)
2723
2724 proc `+`*(time: Time, interval: TimeInterval): Time =
2725 ## Adds `interval` to `time`.
2726 ## If `interval` contains any years, months, weeks or days the operation
2727 ## is performed in the local timezone.
2728 runnableExamples:
2729 let tm = fromUnix(0)
2730 doAssert tm + 5.seconds == fromUnix(5)
2731
2732 if interval.isStaticInterval:
2733 time + evaluateStaticInterval(interval)
2734 else:
2735 toTime(time.local + interval)
2736
2737 proc `-`*(time: Time, interval: TimeInterval): Time =
2738 ## Subtracts `interval` from Time `time`.
2739 ## If `interval` contains any years, months, weeks or days the operation
2740 ## is performed in the local timezone.
2741 runnableExamples:
2742 let tm = fromUnix(5)
2743 doAssert tm - 5.seconds == fromUnix(0)
2744
2745 if interval.isStaticInterval:
2746 time - evaluateStaticInterval(interval)
2747 else:
2748 toTime(time.local - interval)
2749
2750 proc `+=`*(a: var DateTime, b: TimeInterval) =
2751 a = a + b
2752
2753 proc `-=`*(a: var DateTime, b: TimeInterval) =
2754 a = a - b
2755
2756 proc `+=`*(t: var Time, b: TimeInterval) =
2757 t = t + b
2758
2759 proc `-=`*(t: var Time, b: TimeInterval) =
2760 t = t - b
2761
2762 #
2763 # Iso week
2764 #
2765
2766 proc initDateTime*(weekday: WeekDay, isoweek: IsoWeekRange, isoyear: IsoYear,
2767 hour: HourRange, minute: MinuteRange, second: SecondRange,
2768 nanosecond: NanosecondRange,
2769 zone: Timezone = local()): DateTime {.raises: [], tags: [], since: (1, 5).} =
2770 ## Create a new `DateTime <#DateTime>`_ from a weekday and an ISO 8601 week number and year
2771 ## in the specified timezone.
2772 ##
2773 ## .. warning:: The ISO week-based year can correspond to the following or previous year from 29 December to January 3.
2774 runnableExamples:
2775 assert initDateTime(21, mApr, 2018, 00, 00, 00) == initDateTime(dSat, 16, 2018.IsoYear, 00, 00, 00)
2776 assert initDateTime(30, mDec, 2019, 00, 00, 00) == initDateTime(dMon, 01, 2020.IsoYear, 00, 00, 00)
2777 assert initDateTime(13, mSep, 2020, 00, 00, 00) == initDateTime(dSun, 37, 2020.IsoYear, 00, 00, 00)
2778 assert initDateTime(2, mJan, 2021, 00, 00, 00) == initDateTime(dSat, 53, 2020.IsoYear, 00, 00, 00)
2779
2780 # source https://webspace.science.uu.nl/~gent0113/calendar/isocalendar.htm
2781 let d = isoweek * 7 + weekday.int - initDateTime(4, mJan, isoyear.int, 00, 00, 00, zone).weekday.int - 4
2782 initDateTime(1, mJan, isoyear.int, hour, minute, second, nanosecond, zone) + initTimeInterval(days=d)
2783
2784 proc initDateTime*(weekday: WeekDay, isoweek: IsoWeekRange, isoyear: IsoYear,
2785 hour: HourRange, minute: MinuteRange, second: SecondRange,
2786 zone: Timezone = local()): DateTime {.raises: [], tags: [], since: (1, 5).} =
2787 initDateTime(weekday, isoweek, isoyear, hour, minute, second, 0, zone)
2788
2789 #
2790 # Other
2791 #
2792
2793 proc epochTime*(): float {.tags: [TimeEffect].} =
2794 ## Gets time after the UNIX epoch (1970) in seconds. It is a float
2795 ## because sub-second resolution is likely to be supported (depending
2796 ## on the hardware/OS).
2797 ##
2798 ## `getTime` should generally be preferred over this proc.
2799 ##
2800 ## .. warning:: Unsuitable for benchmarking (but still better than `now`),
2801 ## use `monotimes.getMonoTime` or `cpuTime` instead, depending on the use case.
2802 when defined(js):
2803 result = newDate().getTime() / 1000
2804 elif defined(macosx):
2805 var a {.noinit.}: Timeval
2806 gettimeofday(a)
2807 result = toBiggestFloat(a.tv_sec.int64) + toBiggestFloat(
2808 a.tv_usec)*0.00_0001
2809 elif defined(posix):
2810 var ts {.noinit.}: Timespec
2811 discard clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, ts)
2812 result = toBiggestFloat(ts.tv_sec.int64) +
2813 toBiggestFloat(ts.tv_nsec.int64) / 1_000_000_000
2814 elif defined(windows):
2815 var f {.noinit.}: winlean.FILETIME
2816 getSystemTimeAsFileTime(f)
2817 var i64 = rdFileTime(f) - epochDiff
2818 var secs = i64 div rateDiff
2819 var subsecs = i64 mod rateDiff
2820 result = toFloat(int(secs)) + toFloat(int(subsecs)) * 0.0000001
2821 else:
2822 {.error: "unknown OS".}
2823
2824 when not defined(js):
2825 type
2826 Clock {.importc: "clock_t".} = distinct int
2827
2828 proc getClock(): Clock
2829 {.importc: "clock", header: "<time.h>", tags: [TimeEffect], used, sideEffect.}
2830
2831 var
2832 clocksPerSec {.importc: "CLOCKS_PER_SEC", nodecl, used.}: int
2833
2834 proc cpuTime*(): float {.tags: [TimeEffect].} =
2835 ## Gets time spent that the CPU spent to run the current process in
2836 ## seconds. This may be more useful for benchmarking than `epochTime`.
2837 ## However, it may measure the real time instead (depending on the OS).
2838 ## The value of the result has no meaning.
2839 ## To generate useful timing values, take the difference between
2840 ## the results of two `cpuTime` calls:
2841 runnableExamples:
2842 var t0 = cpuTime()
2843 # some useless work here (calculate fibonacci)
2844 var fib = @[0, 1, 1]
2845 for i in 1..10:
2846 fib.add(fib[^1] + fib[^2])
2847 echo "CPU time [s] ", cpuTime() - t0
2848 echo "Fib is [s] ", fib
2849 ## When the flag `--benchmarkVM` is passed to the compiler, this proc is
2850 ## also available at compile time
2851 when defined(posix) and not defined(osx) and declared(CLOCK_THREAD_CPUTIME_ID):
2852 # 'clocksPerSec' is a compile-time constant, possibly a
2853 # rather awful one, so use clock_gettime instead
2854 var ts: Timespec = default(Timespec)
2855 discard clock_gettime(CLOCK_THREAD_CPUTIME_ID, ts)
2856 result = toFloat(ts.tv_sec.int) +
2857 toFloat(ts.tv_nsec.int) / 1_000_000_000
2858 else:
2859 result = toFloat(int(getClock())) / toFloat(clocksPerSec)
2860
2861
2862 #
2863 # Deprecations
2864 #
2865
2866 proc `nanosecond=`*(dt: var DateTime, value: NanosecondRange) {.deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.3.1".} =
2867 dt.nanosecond = value
2868
2869 proc `second=`*(dt: var DateTime, value: SecondRange) {.deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.3.1".} =
2870 dt.second = value
2871
2872 proc `minute=`*(dt: var DateTime, value: MinuteRange) {.deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.3.1".} =
2873 dt.minute = value
2874
2875 proc `hour=`*(dt: var DateTime, value: HourRange) {.deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.3.1".} =
2876 dt.hour = value
2877
2878 proc `monthdayZero=`*(dt: var DateTime, value: int) {.deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.3.1".} =
2879 dt.monthdayZero = value
2880
2881 proc `monthZero=`*(dt: var DateTime, value: int) {.deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.3.1".} =
2882 dt.monthZero = value
2883
2884 proc `year=`*(dt: var DateTime, value: int) {.deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.3.1".} =
2885 dt.year = value
2886
2887 proc `weekday=`*(dt: var DateTime, value: WeekDay) {.deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.3.1".} =
2888 dt.weekday = value
2889
2890 proc `yearday=`*(dt: var DateTime, value: YeardayRange) {.deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.3.1".} =
2891 dt.yearday = value
2892
2893 proc `isDst=`*(dt: var DateTime, value: bool) {.deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.3.1".} =
2894 dt.isDst = value
2895
2896 proc `timezone=`*(dt: var DateTime, value: Timezone) {.deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.3.1".} =
2897 dt.timezone = value
2898
2899 proc `utcOffset=`*(dt: var DateTime, value: int) {.deprecated: "Deprecated since v1.3.1".} =
2900 dt.utcOffset = value
2901
